files_to_change.change: Add refs int field to fileSplitState; replace non-refcounted stateFor accessor with acquireGuard/releaseGuard (refcounted, mirroring btree/latch.go's acquireLatch/releaseLatch) plus a plain-lookup helper for Release's no-op-on-miss contract; rewire TryAcquire/Release/InProgress on top of the new accessors; add guardRegistrySize() test-only helper; update doc comments (struct-level growth-characteristic note, method-level ordering rationale).

Add TestFileGuardRegistryBounded (guard a large number of distinct fileIDs, acquire+release each, assert registry size stays bounded); no changes to existing tests (they must keep passing against the new implementation unchanged).

files_explicitly_not_touched: engine/catalog/content.go (4.5.3.1, deferred to a later run per launch instructions)
engine/catalog/* (other than the explicit exclusion above)

callers_of_FileGuard_checked.method: grep for FileGuard, TryAcquire, Release(, InProgress( usages outside guard.go/guard_test.go
callers_of_FileGuard_checked.result: no other production call sites yet in this tree (orchestrate.go wiring is 4.5.3.3's/later scope); public API signatures (NewFileGuard, TryAcquire(fileID uint64) bool, Release(fileID uint64), InProgress(fileID uint64) bool) are preserved unchanged, so no caller-side changes are needed regardless.

risk_level: medium — concurrency-sensitive change to a locking/CAS primitive, but no external callers yet in this tree reduces blast radius
concurrency_invariants_to_preserve: Exactly one TryAcquire winner per fileID at any given time (no double-acquisition), including across an eviction+recreation cycle.
No refcount leaks on the TryAcquire-failure (losing) path.
Release must store false before releasing/evicting the ref, not after (see architecture-discovery.md for the double-winner hazard this prevents).
Release remains a documented no-op (no panic) when called on a fileID that was never acquired or already fully released/evicted.
